title: Scheduled Dictionary Object Refresh and Change Notifications
story: As a product administrator, I want to schedule automatic refreshes of my Data Dictionary objects and receive email notifications when changes occur, so that I can monitor how my Salesforce org is evolving on a daily basis without manual checks.
description:
Introduces a scheduled refresh and change notification system for Data Dictionary objects. Administrators can opt specific objects into a daily refresh schedule. When changes are detected, email notifications are sent via SendGrid to subscribed users (must hold DataTools seats).

A settings page allows users to:
- Add objects to the scheduler from their configured dictionary objects
- Remove objects from the scheduler
- View which objects are currently scheduled

The scheduler runs as a Laravel cron job once daily at midnight. Only opted-in users receive notifications.

This feature is positioned as an enterprise capability, pairing with scheduled report tracking to justify higher-tier pricing.
acceptance_criteria: Settings page shows an 'Add New Object to Scheduler' button
Button displays available dictionary objects not yet in the scheduler
Users can add and remove objects from the scheduler
A Laravel cron job runs daily at midnight to refresh scheduled objects
When changes are detected during refresh, email notifications are sent via SendGrid
Only subscribed users with DataTools seats receive notifications
Non-subscribed users see a prompt to subscribe when attempting to add objects
ux_changes: New settings page section for managing scheduled dictionary objects. Shows current scheduled objects, add/remove controls, and a subscription prompt for non-subscribed users via a slide-out panel.
middleware_changes: Laravel cron job that runs daily to refresh Salesforce object metadata for scheduled objects. Detects changes and triggers SendGrid email notifications to subscribed team members.
data_model_changes: New storage for scheduler configuration: which objects are scheduled, subscriber list per team. Tracks refresh history and detected changes.
api_changes: Endpoints for adding/removing objects from the scheduler, retrieving current schedule, and triggering manual refresh.
implementation_notes: Uses Laravel's built-in scheduler for cron management. SendGrid handles email delivery. The cron runs at midnight daily. This is considered an enterprise feature — the combination of scheduled object refresh and report tracking supports premium pricing tiers.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the scheduler with add/remove UX, Laravel cron job, and SendGrid notifications. Tested locally before deploying to development and production. Cron configured to run once daily at midnight.
